steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: The sentence says the question of how people 27,000 years ago understood the Gargas cave paintings “is ______: we will never be able to answer it.” The colon signals that the blank is defined by what follows: the blank must mean unable to be answered.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘irresolvable’: means impossible to settle or answer — matches the colon’s explanation that we can never answer it. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘self-contradictory’: means containing internal contradictions — the question isn’t logically contradictory; it is simply unanswerable because no evidence survives. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘imperative’: means urgently necessary — the passage expresses pessimism about ever answering the question, not urgency about doing so. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘unavoidable’: means impossible to escape — a question can be unavoidable yet still answerable; this does not capture “we will never be able to answer it.” ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
